# Work Packet ALB-025 — One-Command Fresh-Machine Installer

## 1. TASK

A `./install.sh` that takes a freshly-cloned Albion checkout to a working install in under five minutes: verify prerequisites, put the `bin/` tools on PATH, and confirm health — without ever handling the user's secret token.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME

- `install.sh` (repo root, executable).
- `tests/test_install.sh` — hermetic tests.
- A short "Install" section added near the top of `README.md` (the ONE sanctioned edit outside the two files above).

## 3. CONTEXT

- **Read first:** `bin/albion` (how it resolves repo root — the symlinks must preserve that; it uses `resolve_script_dir` walking symlinks, so symlinking the launcher into a PATH dir must still resolve back to the repo), `bin/albion-doctor`, `env/albion-env.sh` (token variables — the installer must NOT read or write them), `CONVENTIONS.md` (portability: macOS ships bash 3.2 — the installer must run under it), `README.md` (Quickstart section for tone).
- **Contract:** `./install.sh [--prefix DIR] [--no-doctor] [--help]`.
  - Prereq checks (fail with a clear message naming the fix): `bash`, `python3`, `curl`, `git` present; `claude` on PATH and >= 2.1.163 (WARN, do not fail, if 2.1.163–2.1.194; FAIL below 2.1.163; WARN if absent — Albion can install but not run).
  - PATH install: symlink `albion`, `albion-doctor`, `albion-vision`, `albion-compile` from `bin/` into `${PREFIX:-$HOME/.local/bin}` (create it if absent). Use absolute symlink targets so the launcher's symlink-walk resolves the repo root. Overwrite existing Albion symlinks (idempotent); refuse to overwrite a non-symlink or a symlink pointing outside this repo (print what it found, skip it, keep going).
  - If the prefix dir is not on `PATH`, print the exact line to add to the shell profile (do not edit profiles automatically).
  - Token: print concise guidance to set `ALBION_ZAI_TOKEN` (plan lane) or `ALBION_ZAI_API_KEY` (api lane) before first run. **Never read, echo, prompt for, or write a token.**
  - Unless `--no-doctor`, finish by running `bin/albion-doctor` (offline) and echoing its summary line; installer exit status reflects doctor's only if doctor was run, else 0 on successful symlinking.
- **Portability:** bash 3.2-safe (no associative arrays, no `${var,,}`); `python3` for version parsing if needed; no GNU-only flags.

## 4. MUST DO

- Idempotent: a second run makes no destructive change and exits 0.
- Absolute, repo-rooted symlink targets; verify (in the script) that a resolved `albion` symlink still points inside the repo before reporting success.
- `tests/test_install.sh`, wired into `bash tests/run.sh`: run `install.sh --prefix <tempdir> --no-doctor` with a stubbed PATH providing a fake `claude --version` (reuse the pattern in `tests/test_doctor.sh`); assert all four symlinks created and resolve to the repo's `bin/`; assert a second run is clean and still exit 0; assert `--help` prints usage and exits 0; assert a missing-prereq case (PATH without `python3`) fails with a message naming `python3`; assert a below-minimum `claude --version` is handled per contract; assert no token env var is ever read (grep the script for `ALBION_ZAI` shows only comments/guidance, never a read into a variable). Everything hermetic — temp prefix, temp PATH, no writes to the real `$HOME` or repo.
- CI-equivalent shellcheck clean; run the full suite yourself before finishing.

## 5. MUST NOT DO

- Do NOT read, prompt for, store, or transmit any token or secret.
- Do NOT edit shell profiles, `~/.claude/settings.json`, or anything under `$HOME` outside the chosen prefix dir.
- Do NOT fetch anything from the network (no curl to remote installers).
- Do NOT modify `bin/`, `plugin/`, `env/`, `state/`, or any test other than the new one.
- Do NOT modify anything outside the three sanctioned paths. If an out-of-scope test breaks, STOP and report.
- Do NOT run git state-changing commands.

## 6. TOOLS ALLOWED

File creation/editing and shell execution within the repository workspace only.

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] `bash tests/run.sh` fully green including `test_install.sh`.
- [ ] `./install.sh --prefix /tmp/x --no-doctor` symlinks the four tools; re-run is clean; symlinked `albion` still resolves the repo root.
- [ ] Missing/old prerequisites produce clear, actionable messages; no token is ever touched.
- [ ] CI-equivalent shellcheck clean; `git status` shows only the three allowed paths.
